mission: Teach agents to reason about coalescing, irregular access, shared-memory reuse, halo loading, vectorized loads, and layout conversion before claiming custom memory-kernel wins.

must_not_claim: rocprofiler/rocprof sector/request evidence without profile artifacts
architecture-general bandwidth wins from one GPU timing
library-unbeatable conclusions without scoped custom-kernel hypotheses

tasks.objective: Compare irregular index-map gather/scatter with a coalesced identity-map path that can use vectorized loads and stores.
custom_hypotheses: Identity or affine maps can skip index reads and use coalesced vectorized memory operations.
Arbitrary permutations are likely memory-transaction and index-stream bound unless the workload permits reordering or preprocessing.
required_evidence_before_claim: mode recorded as irregular or coalesced
HIP-event timing label or profiler artifacts
correctness pass for gathered and scattered outputs

tasks.objective: Measure whether a shared-memory halo tile reduces redundant global loads enough to beat a simple global-memory stencil for one pass.
custom_hypotheses: Shared halo reuse should help when neighboring cells are reused across threads.
For a single lightweight pass, synchronization and halo loading overhead can erase the benefit, making a negative example useful.
required_evidence_before_claim: HIP-event timing label or profiler artifacts
correctness pass against CPU stencil reference

tasks.objective: Convert x/y/z/w AoS tuples into SoA arrays while testing whether vectorized tuple loads improve a mostly bandwidth-bound layout transform.
custom_hypotheses: A 16-byte aligned AoS input can be loaded as float4 while preserving coalesced writes to each SoA output.
Vectorized loads may be neutral if stores dominate or if alignment and register pressure offset transaction savings.
required_evidence_before_claim: HIP-event timing label or profiler artifacts
correctness pass for all four output arrays
